content: If we reframe the curiosity-driven internal reward mechanism of a single autonomous agent as a lightweight A2A interaction between the agent's current exploratory state and its past memory stored as a separate curiosity agent, we can build a more efficient self-improving agent system that does not require external networked A2A infrastructure. This turns intra-agent memory and motivation into a native distributed agent-to-agent protocol, eliminating the need for redundant cross-device communication overhead for most self-improvement tasks.
